owasp_api_security: API3:2023 - Broken Object Property Level Authorization
cwe: CWE-639 - Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
CWE-200 - Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor

threat.title: API Endpoint Enumeration and Information Disclosure via Sequential Object IDs
threat.description: REST APIs that use sequential, predictable object identifiers (integers, incremental IDs) without proper authorization checks allow attackers to enumerate all resources by iterating through ID values. This vulnerability enables mass data harvesting, user enumeration, and exposure of sensitive information through systematic API requests. The attack exploits the combination of predictable IDs and missing object-level authorization controls.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker authenticates with valid credentials and receives access to /api/v1/users/1234 (their own profile)
2. Observes that user ID is a sequential integer in the API endpoint path
3. Tests access to /api/v1/users/1233 and /api/v1/users/1235 without authorization checks
4. Discovers API returns full user data (email, phone, address) for any valid user ID
5. Develops automated script to enumerate all users from /api/v1/users/1 to /api/v1/users/50000
6. Harvests 50,000 user records containing PII (names, emails, phone numbers, addresses) within 10 minutes
7. Exports data for spam campaigns, phishing attacks, or sale on dark web marketplaces
prerequisites: API uses sequential or predictable object identifiers (integers, incrementing values)
Missing or insufficient authorization checks on object-level access
API returns sensitive information without proper filtering
No rate limiting or enumeration protection mechanisms implemented

cve_references.description: User enumeration via API endpoint in Indico - sequential user IDs allowed bulk disclosure of user details

real_world_breaches.impact: 70TB of data including posts, videos, and user information scraped via sequential post IDs
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Sequential post IDs allowed systematic enumeration of all content without authorization

real_world_breaches.impact: Unauthorized team and organization creation via sequential numerical user IDs (e.g., 123231405)
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Inadequate validation of userId parameters in POST requests combined with enumerable sequential IDs

real_world_breaches.impact: Information disclosure vulnerability allowing remote enumeration of usernames via REST API
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Publicly exposed /wp-json/wp/v2/users endpoint with sequential user IDs

bug_bounty_reports.summary: IDOR in API applications allowing enumeration and disclosure of user data via sequential IDs
Access to mod logs from any public or restricted subreddit via IDOR with sequential log IDs
IDOR allowing disclosure of Username, Email, PIN, FirstName, LastName, Phone for 1,700+ users via sequential IDs

security_research.title: API1:2023 Broken Object Level Authorization
security_research.key_finding: BOLA is the #1 API security risk in 2023, present in ~40% of all API attacks. Sequential IDs combined with missing authorization enable systematic enumeration and mass data exposure.

security_research.title: REST Security Cheat Sheet
security_research.key_finding: Non-public REST services must perform access control at each API endpoint. Sequential identifiers significantly increase enumeration risk.

security_research.title: API Security in 2024: Beyond the Crystal Ball
security_research.key_finding: API endpoint enumeration remains a fundamental step in assessing API attack surface. Proper inventory and authorization are critical defenses.

mitigations.title: Implement Rate Limiting and Enumeration Detection
mitigations.description: Deploy rate limiting at both application and infrastructure layers to detect and block systematic enumeration attempts. Configure progressive rate limits that become stricter as suspicious patterns emerge, and implement CAPTCHA challenges for repeated access patterns.

tools.name: Redis
tools.type: In-memory data store for rate limiting

tools.name: Azure API Management
tools.type: API Gateway with built-in rate limiting

nist_csf: DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
validation_steps: Deploy rate limiting with 100 req/min per IP baseline
Configure enumeration detection for 20+ unique resource accesses in 5 min
Test with enumeration script to verify blocking triggers
Verify legitimate users can access their own resources without issues
Monitor rate limit metrics in Application Insights or Azure Monitor

mitigations.title: Replace Sequential IDs with UUIDs or Non-Enumerable Identifiers
mitigations.description: Migrate from sequential integer IDs to UUIDs (Universally Unique Identifiers) or cryptographically random identifiers that cannot be predicted or enumerated. This eliminates the ability for attackers to systematically iterate through all resources.
nist_csf: PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
PR.AC-4 - Access permissions and authorizations are managed
validation_steps: Add UUID column to database tables
Migrate existing records to include UUIDs
Update API endpoints to accept UUID parameters
Add UUID format validation to reject invalid IDs
Test enumeration attempts to verify UUIDs prevent systematic iteration
Update API documentation with new ID format
Monitor for 400 errors indicating UUID validation failures

mitigations.title: Implement Object-Level Authorization Checks
mitigations.description: Enforce authorization validation for every API request to ensure users can only access resources they own or have explicit permission to view. Never rely solely on obscurity of IDs; always verify the requesting user has rights to the requested object.

tools.name: Azure AD B2C
tools.cost: First 50,000 MAU free, then $0.00325 per MAU

iso27001_controls: A.9.2.3 - Management of privileged access rights
nist_csf: PR.AC-4 - Access permissions and authorizations are managed
PR.PT-3 - The principle of least functionality is incorporated
validation_steps: Add authorization checks to all API endpoints returning user data
Verify users cannot access other users' resources via direct ID manipulation
Test with different user roles (regular, admin, support)
Log all authorization failures for security monitoring
Implement automated tests for authorization logic
Review authorization logic during code reviews
Monitor for spikes in 403 Forbidden responses indicating attack attempts

indicators: High volume of 403 Forbidden responses from a single IP address
Sequential or incremental patterns in requested resource IDs from the same source
Rapid requests to the same endpoint with varying ID parameters
High ratio of 404 Not Found responses indicating ID probing
Access to resources outside of normal user behavior patterns
Single user/IP accessing resources belonging to multiple different users

log_queries.query:
// Detect API enumeration patterns
ApiManagementGatewayLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(5m)
| where Url contains "/api/v1/users/" or Url contains "/api/v1/orders/"
| extend ResourceId = extract(@"/users/(\\d+)", 1, Url)
| extend ClientIP = tostring(parse_json(RequestHeaders)["X-Forwarded-For"])
| summarize 
    RequestCount = count(),
    UniqueResourceIds = dcount(ResourceId),
    ForbiddenCount = countif(ResponseCode == 403),
    NotFoundCount = countif(ResponseCode == 404)
    by ClientIP, bin(TimeGenerated, 1m)
| where UniqueResourceIds > 20 or (ForbiddenCount > 10) or (RequestCount > 100)
| project TimeGenerated, ClientIP, RequestCount, UniqueResourceIds, ForbiddenCount, NotFoundCount
| order by UniqueResourceIds desc

// Detect enumeration attempts
http.response.status_code:(403 OR 404) AND url.path:/\\/api\\/v[0-9]+\\/(users|orders|documents)\\/[0-9]+/
| stats count() as total_requests, 
        cardinality(url.path) as unique_resources,
        sum(case(http.response.status_code == 403, 1, 0)) as forbidden_count
        by source.ip, bucket(span=1m)
| where unique_resources > 20 OR forbidden_count > 10 OR total_requests > 100

index=api_logs sourcetype=rest_api
| rex field=url "/api/v\\d+/(?<resource_type>users|orders|documents)/(?<resource_id>\\d+)"
| where isnotnull(resource_id)
| stats count as total_requests,
        dc(resource_id) as unique_resources,
        count(where(status==403)) as forbidden_count,
        count(where(status==404)) as not_found_count
        by client_ip, resource_type
| where unique_resources > 20 OR forbidden_count > 10 OR total_requests > 100
| sort - unique_resources
| addinfo
| table _time, client_ip, resource_type, total_requests, unique_resources, forbidden_count, not_found_count

requests
| where timestamp > ago(5m)
| where url matches regex @"/api/v\\d+/(users|orders|documents)/(\\d+|[a-f0-9-]{36})"
| extend resource_type = extract(@"/api/v\\d+/(\\w+)/", 1, url)
| extend resource_id = extract(@"/(\\d+|[a-f0-9-]{36})$", 1, url)
| summarize 
    total_requests = count(),
    unique_ids = dcount(resource_id),
    forbidden = countif(resultCode == 403),
    not_found = countif(resultCode == 404),
    avg_duration = avg(duration)
    by client_IP, resource_type, bin(timestamp, 1m)
| where unique_ids > 20 or forbidden > 10
| project timestamp, client_IP, resource_type, total_requests, unique_ids, forbidden, not_found, avg_duration
| order by unique_ids desc

tools.name: Azure API Management Analytics
tools.usage: Monitor request patterns, response codes, and anomalous behavior in real-time

tools.name: Burp Suite Intruder
tools.usage: Simulate enumeration attacks to test rate limiting and authorization controls

tools.name: Azure Sentinel
tools.usage: Create automated playbooks to block IPs exhibiting enumeration patterns
